parsed_review.summary: CP00-805 (Risk C, 150 units, RP26.P05.M06.S19-RP26.P06.M02.S20) is a sound descriptor-only continuation of the established CP788-CP804 enterprise-SaaS hardening pattern. Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack is honest and not overclaiming: manifest declares production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=in_progress (the correct pre-closeout state; closed CP804 ends at production_ready=true/runtime_ready=false). I recomputed the coverage from the live plan included_units and it matches exactly: 150 units; deliverable distribution implementation 58 / fixture 28 / claude_review 7 / security_audit 23 / test 18 / hermes_evidence 4 / ui 12; phase counts RP26.P05=90, RP26.P06=60; and the registry row constants (FIXTURE_GOLDEN_ROWS, PERMISSION_MATRIX_ROWS incl. slices for M06/M10) match the plan unit titles per micro-phase. Boundaries hold throughout: ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is spread into every result; every case-set row is descriptor_only=true / runtime_execution=false with no tenant/SSO/SCIM/secret payloads; validators (validators.js) and tests (model.test.js) actively reject runtime permission evaluation, permission-decision/audit writes, UI/API runtime, real tenant data, cross-tenant routes, key/secret material, and enterprise-trust claims. SSO/SCIM modules are descriptor-only with all runtime flags false. Authority boundary is correct: Claude is read_only + non-final (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), human approval required for runtime opening; Hermes packets are descriptor-only with emits_runtime_receipt=false (no product-runtime Hermes). Contract projection promotes CP805 to latest with prior packs historical and validation.valid=true; the validate script targets CP805. No secrets/PII found in source or the generated contract. The review readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON), and there is no pre-existing invalid review evidence to reject (raw-output/receipt are produced by this run). changed-file-scope is clean; unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room UI, LDIP, workbook docs) are correctly excluded from pack scope. One non-blocking P3 transparency caveat: the downstream closeout evidence artifacts (command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) and green executions of the listed validation commands are produced by/after this read-only review and could not be directly observed here; the production_ready flip must wait for them and for npm run closeout-pack:validate to pass. No P0/P1 issues; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Downstream closeout evidence artifacts and dynamic validation runs not directly observable under read-only review
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts lists command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json currently exists in docs/closeout-packs/cp00-805/ and raw-output.json/review-receipt.json are not yet present in artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-805/. This is the expected state at pack_level_claude_review.status=review_pending: these files are produced by/after this Claude review during closeout finalization. Because the review is strictly read-only (Read/Grep/Glob, no Bash), the actual results of the validation commands (npm test, npm run validate, closeout-pack:validate, etc.) and the adjudication/inspection documents could not be directly executed or observed. This PASS is therefore a static-inspection PASS: the implementation, validators, tests, contract projection, and no-write/no-real-data/runtime-closed boundaries are internally consistent and correct as written.
findings.recommendation: During closeout finalization, generate the four required pack artifacts and confirm all listed validation commands (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) exit 0 with valid evidence before flipping production_ready to true and committing. No code change required for this pack.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected pre-closeout state, not a defect in the pack. Non-blocking; will be satisfied by the deterministic closeout pipeline that consumes this review receipt. Does not block pack or goal closeout.
